summary: Narrative memory retrieval layer — SQLite FTS5 index over session-log, decisions, learnings, snapshots, session-state, events + session-search skill + context budget audit tool.
notes: Safe overlay. The new memory.db lives under .phantom/ and is fully rebuildable from source files (idempotent). No existing data is migrated or touched. FTS5 requires the SQLite build shipped with Python stdlib on macOS / Debian-based Linux (standard). If FTS5 is unavailable, only the session-search skill fails — core workspace unaffected.

changes.note: Indexer. Parses session-log.md, decisions.md, brands/{slug}/learnings.json, _snapshot.md, session-state.md, .phantom/context-engine-events.jsonl. Rebuilds .phantom/memory.db + FTS5 virtual table.
Query CLI. --query / --type / --brand / --since / --limit / --format. FTS5 auto-escaped. Returns ranked chunks with snippet + source.
New navigator skill, type=navigator, subagent_safe=true, recommended_model=haiku. FR/EN triggers on history queries. Disambiguates against query-context.
Maintainer tool. Measures CLAUDE.md cascade lines + lazy-loaded docs, reports against thresholds (root ≤140, always-loaded ≤250, lazy ≤200, worst-case ≤600). --strict for CI gate.
Regenerated to pick up session-search (30 skills total, was 29).
